hero.headline: Record your agent. Replay byte-identical, every time.

problem.body: Every agent run is a one-way trip. The LLM call was non-deterministic, the clock read a different value, the third-party API response has drifted. When a bug surfaces in production you have logs — but logs are not a replay. You can’t re-run the agent’s logic against what actually happened.

proposal.body: AgentReplay is a primitive for deterministic agent replay. Wrap your boundary calls in the recorder, get back a portable trace. Replay it and the agent re-executes against the recorded outputs — byte-identical final output, byte-identical step sequence. Open spec, MIT-licensed, reference CLI included.

whyNow.body: Agents are shipping to production. Debugging them is still pre-industrial: re-run with the same prompt and hope for the same result. The boundary-recording pattern that makes replay possible has no standard packaging. AgentReplay is that packaging — a spec and a reference implementation any agent author can adopt today.

about.body: Built by Aiden as part of the Accuoa AI-agent infrastructure portfolio. Composes with agent-reputation (Plan 8, signed records), p2p-agent-discovery (Plan 9, manifest evidence), and memorystore (Plan 1, memory boundaries).
